2016-06-25.title: European Leaders Tell a Dazed Britain to Get Going on ‘Brexit’
2016-06-25.abstract: There were clear indications Saturday of a tense and bickering divorce, as European leaders urged Britain to hurry up and begin its departure.

2016-06-25.title: After ‘Brexit,’ 3 Centuries of Unity in Britain Are in Danger
2016-06-25.abstract: The United Kingdom is what the European Union aspired to be but never became: a multinational state. The vote to leave the union may push Scotland and Northern Ireland to undo that arrangement.

2016-06-26.title: India’s ‘Brexit’ Took More Than a Referendum
2016-06-26.abstract: In the aftermath of Britain’s decision to leave the European Union, Indians reflect on the long road to their own independence and unification.

2016-06-26.title: Fractures From ‘Brexit’ Vote Spread Into Opposition Labour Party
2016-06-26.abstract: Internal criticism of the leader, Jeremy Corbyn, led to his firing the shadow foreign secretary, and at least 11 other members of the party’s cabinet resigned.

2016-06-26.title: Tough Choices and Hard Lessons for E.U. After ‘Brexit’ Vote
2016-06-26.abstract: An altered power dynamic must be addressed as well as the question of whether to make fundamental changes or push ahead with deeper integration.

2016-06-26.title: Chinese Activist Chen Yunfei Is to Stand Trial, Lawyer Says
2016-06-26.abstract: Mr. Chen, an artist, has been detained for more than a year after visiting the grave site of a victim of the Tiananmen Square crackdown.

2016-06-26.title: 1891: A Platonic Strike in Paris
2016-06-26.abstract: From the archives of The International Herald Tribune: In June 1891, a general strike of the journeymen bakers of Paris proved to be platonic.

2016-06-27.title: Israel and Turkey Agree to Resume Full Diplomatic Ties
2016-06-27.abstract: The two countries, once close regional allies, fell out after a deadly confrontation in 2010. They are scheduled to announce a deal to resume relations in Rome on Monday.

2016-06-27.title: Election in Spain Gives Conservatives More Seats but No Mandate
2016-06-27.abstract: The conservative Popular Party won 137 of 350 seats in Parliament, up from 123 in the December elections but not enough to settle who will form the next government.

2016-06-27.title: Demand for ‘Himalayan Viagra’ Fungus Heats Up, Maybe Too Much
2016-06-27.abstract: Caterpillar fungus, hailed as an aphrodisiac, can top $50,000 a pound and is an economic mainstay for mountain dwellers in Nepal, but it is becoming more scarce.

2016-06-27.title: With ‘Brexit,’ Washington’s Direct Line to the Continent Suddenly Frays
2016-06-27.abstract: Officials say no country shares the United States’ worldview quite the way Britain does in terms of security, intelligence and trade.

2016-06-27.title: Panama Celebrates Expanded Canal’s Successful First Passage
2016-06-27.abstract: Seventy heads of state were invited to watch as a container ship made it through without a hitch, though an unrelated accident involving two vessels outside the locks sent four people to a hospital.

2016-06-28.title: Climate Change High on Agenda as Obama and Trudeau Meet for Summit
2016-06-28.abstract: Leaders of the United States, Mexico and Canada will announce a climate partnership, synchronizing their national policies to cut greenhouse gas emissions.

2016-06-28.title: Unicef Says Its Development Goals for Children Are at Risk
2016-06-28.abstract: The United Nations children’s agency projected that by 2030, almost 70 million children will have died from preventable causes.

2016-06-28.title: Elaborate Suicide Attack Hits Christian Village in Lebanon
2016-06-28.abstract: While bombings have hit Beirut and elsewhere during the Syrian war, such assaults have been rare in the area of Monday’s attack.

2016-06-28.title: Order of Business for E.U. in Brussels? Weeds, Then ‘Brexit’
2016-06-28.abstract: As leaders gather for the first time since Britain’s vote, regulatory minutiae will come first, illustrating why so many view the bloc as a meddling bureaucratic machine.

2016-06-28.title: It’s Dogs vs. Poachers in the Fight to Protect Elephants
2016-06-28.abstract: Tanzania has trained four Belgian Malinois to check cargo and luggage for smuggled ivory at the main airport and port in Dar es Salaam.

2016-06-29.title: Wave of Terror Attacks in Turkey Continue at a Steady Pace
2016-06-29.abstract: An explosion near a courthouse in Izmir was the latest in a series of deadly attacks in Turkey.

2016-06-29.title: Lured by Jobs in Afghanistan, Nepalis Face Risks and Death
2016-06-29.abstract: An attack shows that South Asian contractors are more vulnerable than many of their Western counterparts.

2016-06-29.title: Escape Tunnel, Dug by Hand, Is Found at Holocaust Massacre Site
2016-06-29.abstract: In 1944, 80 captive Jews who were being forced to burn bodies at a Nazi extermination site attempted an escape.

2016-06-29.title: France’s Latest American Import: Mermaid Schools
2016-06-29.abstract: How would it feel to be Ariel from Disney’s “The Little Mermaid”? Girls who share that fantasy — and some women — are pulling on monofins and finding out.

2016-06-29.title: ‘Brexit’ Briefing: David Cameron Leaves Brussels Early; Conservatives Seek New Leader
2016-06-29.abstract: The British prime minister was excluded from European Union meetings, and Britain’s two main parties are both grappling with leadership changes.

2016-06-30.title: The E.U. Is Democratic. It Just Doesn’t Feel That Way.
2016-06-30.abstract: The bloc was designed to rise above and defuse nationalist politics. That is why, for many Europeans, it does not feel like a functioning democracy.

2016-06-30.title: Turkey, a Conduit for Fighters Joining ISIS, Begins to Feel Its Wrath
2016-06-30.abstract: For years, Turkey served as a rear base, transit hub and shopping bazaar for the Islamic State, but now, officials say Tuesday’s attack bore ISIS hallmarks.

2016-06-30.title: Father Killed in Turkey Attacks Was Trying to Save Son From ISIS
2016-06-30.abstract: His son had called a month after reaching the Islamic State in Syria, asking for help and to return home.

2016-06-30.title: The Battle for Surt
2016-06-30.abstract: Led by militias from nearby Misurata, the attack force has corralled Islamic State fighters into the city center, where they are being pummeled with bombs and gunfire.

2016-06-30.title: A Slow, Steady Siege on an ISIS Stronghold in Libya
2016-06-30.abstract: With a willing commander, a Times correspondent and photographer set off down a highway strewn with the charred remains of vehicles destroyed in suicide bombings.
